H2973.d: properly, to be slack, i.e. (figuratively) to be foolish

H2974.d: properly, to yield, especially assent; hence (pos.) to undertake as an act of volition
H2974.e: a primitive root (probably rather the same as H2973 (יָאַל) through the idea of mental weakness);
H2974.k: assay, begin, be content, please, take upon, [idiom] willingly, would.

H2975.d: a channel, e.g. a fosse, canal, shaft; specifically the Nile, as the one river of Egypt, including its collateral trenches; also the Tigris, as the main river of Assyria

H2976.k: (cause to) despair, one that is desperate, be no hope.

H2977.d: Joshijah, the name of two Israelites
H2977.e: or יֹאשִׁיָּהוּ; from the same root as H803 (אֲשׁוּיָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); founded of Jah;

H2979.e: from the same as H871 (אֲתָרִים); stepping;

H2981.d: produce, i.e. a crop or (figuratively) wealth

H2982.d: Jebus, the original name of Jerusalem

H2983.d: a Jebusite or inhabitant of Jebus

H2985.d: Jabin, the name of two Canaanitish kings

H2986.d: properly, to flow; causatively, to bring (especially with pomp)

H2992.e: a primitive root of doubtful meaning; used only as a denominative from H2993 (יָבָם);
H2992.k: perform the duty of a husband's brother, marry.

H2995.d: Jabneel, the name of two places in Palestine
H2995.e: from H1129 (בָּנָה) and H410 (אֵל); built of God;

H2997.e: from H1129 (בָּנָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); built of Jah;

H2998.e: from H1129 (בָּנָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); building of Jah;
